mnemonics: S-L-O-W: Start to Last, One by One, With patience. Describes the exhaustive nature of sequential searching.
keyPoints: Examines each item in the data structure in a linear sequence until a match is found or the end is reached.
Its time complexity is O(n), making it inefficient for large datasets compared to methods like binary search.
It is simple to implement and does not require the data to be sorted in any particular order.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to check for the end of the list, leading to an out-of-bounds error or infinite loop.
Inefficiently using it on large, sorted datasets where a binary search (O(log n)) would be significantly faster.
